message:
fix(update.sh): correct pgrep pattern for apt lock detection

The original command `pgrep -x apt apt-get dpkg` was incorrect:
- pgrep -x only takes a single pattern
- The remaining arguments (apt-get, dpkg) were being misinterpreted

The fix uses an extended regex pattern with alternation:
  pgrep -x 'apt|apt-get|dpkg'

This correctly matches any of the three process names exactly,
properly detecting when apt/dpkg operations are in progress.

files.patch:
@@ -433,7 +433,7 @@ check_apt_lock() {
     fi
 
     # Check for apt/apt-get processes
-    if pgrep -x apt apt-get dpkg &>/dev/null; then
+    if pgrep -x 'apt|apt-get|dpkg' &>/dev/null; then
         log_item "fail" "apt locked" "apt/dpkg process running"
         log_to_file "APT lock detected: apt/dpkg process already running"
         if [[ "$ABORT_ON_FAILURE" == "true" ]]; then
